patterns_discovered: Riverpod state management pattern with auto-dispose providers
Progress tracking with build spec files
Automated task management with auto-claude system
gotchas_discovered: Need to ensure proper provider auto-dispose to prevent memory leaks
Route configuration must be consistent across app router and feature routes
Spec file updates must maintain proper JSON formatting
Implementation notes require proper Unicode character handling (checkmarks)
File naming conventions require proper dot prefix for hidden files

approach_outcome.method: Feature-first implementation with progressive spec updates
approach_outcome.effectiveness: High - systematic approach with clear progress tracking
approach_outcome.efficiency: Good - used auto-claude system for consistent implementation
approach_outcome.maintainability: High - followed established patterns and conventions
approach_outcome.scalability: High - modular design allows easy feature expansion

recommendations: Continue using the established screen-provider-route pattern for future features
Maintain consistent spec file updates with proper formatting
Ensure all new features follow the same auto-dispose provider pattern
Consider adding automated validation for spec file consistency
Document the architectural patterns for onboarding new developers
Implement automated tests for the sync settings functionality
